implementing emerging technological changes such as integrated AI solutions in our products and services

The rapid evolution and increased adoption of AI technologies may intensify our cybersecurity risks, including risks from malicious or misuse of AI to craft increasingly sophisticated cybersecurity attacks against us.

Hardware, software, AI technology, or applications we develop or obtain from third parties sometimes contain defects in design or deployment or other problems that could unexpectedly result in security breaches.

limitations: No standalone purchase price for BrainBox AI acquisition is disclosed in the excerpts; the $73.9M intangible and $190.0M goodwill figures cover multiple acquisitions combined, limiting AI-attributable capex precision.
AI investment intensity scored 3 rather than 4 because the AI-attributable dollar amounts cannot be cleanly isolated from the combined acquisition figures.
Narrative concreteness scored 4 based on named deployed products and a completed acquisition, but no quantified business results (revenue, efficiency gains) are provided in the excerpts.

Risk disclosure excerpts appear within the business/risk_factors sections; AI risks are named with specific mechanisms (cybersecurity, competitive harm, legal liability) but no quantified risk exposure or named governance body is disclosed, capping score at 4.
